task_completion.visual_evidence: The main window of the Subsurface application is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The dialog or window for editing dive computer nicknames is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A window or dialog box specifically for managing or editing dive computer nicknames (e.g., 'Edit dive computer nicknames') is visible.

task_completion.description: The target dive computer model is located in the list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dive computer model 'Heinrichs Weikamp OSTC 3' is visible in the list of dive computers within the nickname editing dialog.

task_completion.description: The custom nickname is assigned to the target dive computer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'My Primary OSTC' is entered in the nickname field corresponding to the 'Heinrichs Weikamp OSTC 3' dive computer.

task_completion.description: The changes are saved to the dive log.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save' option is clicked in the File menu, a save confirmation is visible, or the main Subsurface window title bar does not show an unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk) after the dialog is closed.

integrity.description: The agent used the Subsurface GUI to make the changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Subsurface application's graphical interface, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not directly edit the dive log file using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) open showing the raw XML/SSRF file contents being modified.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine Subsurface application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application being used has the standard Subsurface layout, menus, and icons.
